{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://kankou-iwaki.or.jp/staff/83128 Iwaki Tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Corridor-type kofun]]

Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>

It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>

The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].

Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].

The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴墓]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴墓]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=corridor-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓}}|横穴式石室|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue ware]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
