{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue pottery]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. ==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue pottery]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=draft}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue pottery]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].

{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of [[Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[Sue pottery]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://kankou-iwaki.or.jp/staff/83128 Iwaki Tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakata Cave Tombs]][[ja:中田横穴]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q18337575]] Wikidata name: Nakata Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[:en:Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://kankou-iwaki.or.jp/staff/83128 Iwaki Tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[:en:Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakata Cave Tombs]][[ja:中田横穴]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18337575]] Wikidata name: Nakata Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[:en:Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakata Cave Tombs]][[ja:中田横穴]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18337575]] Wikidata name: Nakata Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[:en:Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[:en:Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|Iwaki Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].

southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>

It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the [[:en:Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|Iwaki City Archaeological Museum]]<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher=[[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki City]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>

{{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site}} containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima}} in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}} it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the {{ill|Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|en|Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|WD=Q4981824|lt=Iwaki City Archaeological Museum}}<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher={{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki City}} |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|en|Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|WD=Q5353428|lt=Iwaki Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://kankou-iwaki.or.jp/staff/83128 Iwaki Tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakata Cave Tombs]][[ja:中田横穴]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakata Cave Tombs]][[ja:中田横穴]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakata Cave Tombs]][[ja:中田横穴]]
{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Cave Tomb | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Cave Tomb | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Cave Tomb'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site}} containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima}} in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}} it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the {{ill|Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|en|Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|WD=Q4981824|lt=Iwaki City Archaeological Museum}}<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher={{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki City}} |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|en|Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|WD=Q5353428|lt=Iwaki Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://kankou-iwaki.or.jp/staff/83128 Iwaki Tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakata Yokoanabos]][[ja:中田横穴]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakata Yokoanabos]][[ja:中田横穴]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakata Yokoanabos]][[ja:中田横穴]]
{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Yokoanabo | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Yokoanabo | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Yokoanabo'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site}} containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima}} in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Yokoanabo |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}} it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the {{ill|Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|en|Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|WD=Q4981824|lt=Iwaki City Archaeological Museum}}<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher={{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki City}} |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|en|Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|WD=Q5353428|lt=Iwaki Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://kankou-iwaki.or.jp/staff/83128 Iwaki Tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakata Yokoanabos]][[ja:中田横穴]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakata Yokoanabos]][[ja:中田横穴]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nakata Yokoanabos]][[ja:中田横穴]]
{{Short description|Madina Nesipbek}}{{Use mdy dates|date=October 2020}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Nakata Yokoanabo | native_name = 中田横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakata Yokoanabo | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|00|21|N|140|58|01|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = 1969 | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = No | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Nakata Yokoanabo'''|中田横穴| Nakata yokoaka}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the city of {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}} in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is significant in that it is a [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121219205043/http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/3/ |url-status=dead |archive-date=December 19, 2012 |script-title=ja:中田1号横穴 |trans-title=Nakata No. 1 Yokoanabo |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012 }}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/201146/2 |title= 中田横穴|trans-title=Nakata Yokoana |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site is located on a {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} hill approximately five kilometers south of the mouth of the Natsui River and was discovered during construction work on a prefectural road in 1969. The hill contained five cave tombs arranged in three rows, the bottom of which extended for 6.67 meters into the hillside, and was the only one which was decorated. The cave-tomb had a drainage groove and several chambers, each blocked by a stone gate. The decoration is the in form of three rows of continuous lines of red and white triangles, about 40-cm on a side, which covered the rear and side walls of the innermost chamber. Also noteworthy as the fact that the floor had also been painted with the red pigment. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found in a side niche in the tomb included straight swords, bronze bells, fragments of armor, beads, ''[[magatama]]'', rings, horse trappings, bronze cooking vessels and a large amount of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. It is estimated to date from the second half of the sixth century.
It was formerly open to the public for a few days each year; however after damage by the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|1=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}} it has bene closed. Excavated artifacts are exhibited at the {{ill|Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|en|Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|WD=Q4981824|lt=Iwaki City Archaeological Museum|1=Iwaki City Archaeological Museum}}<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |script-title=ja:施設案内「考古資料館」 |trans-title=Facilities: Archaeological Museum |language=Japanese |publisher={{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki City|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}} |accessdate=25 August 2012 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20121020200143/http://www.city.iwaki.fukushima.jp/map/1315/002556.html |archivedate=20 October 2012 }}</ref>
The site is approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|en|Iwaki Station (Fukushima)|WD=Q5353428|lt=Iwaki Station|1=Iwaki Station (Fukushima)}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line|1=Jōban Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://kankou-iwaki.or.jp/staff/83128 Iwaki Tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}
